records.ety: [L. deformare; de- + formare to form, shape, fr. forma: cf. F. dformer. See Form.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deformed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Deforming.]

defs: To spoil the form of; to mar in form; to misshape; to disfigure.
quotes: Deformed, unfinished, sent before my time Into this breathing world.

defs: To render displeasing; to deprive of comeliness, grace, or perfection; to dishonor.
quotes: Above those passions that this world deform.

records.ety: [L. deformis; de- + forma form: cf. OF. deforme, F. difforme. Cf. Difform.]

quotes: Sight so deform what heart of rock could long Dry-eyed behold?
